notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package describes an open battle scene in Madness Cave where multiple parties (Fang Yuan, Le Tu, Giant Sun, Star Constellation) compete for fact icebergs. All described events are publicly observable combat actions - no private knowledge, secret plans, or future-known information is directly supported by the source pointers. The only potential hidden fact would be internal motives/plans, but the source only shows external actions and dialogue, not inner thoughts. No source pointer supports any character knowing something others do not know.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed shows characters collecting fact ice floes and fighting, but observed actions are mostly combat/trade events without clear behavioral patterns that support a cautious one-step motive inference. Multiple characters (Fang Yuan, Le Tu, Giant Yang, Star Constellation) are all competing for fact ice floes - the visible behavior is 'competing for resources' but inferring a specific motive like 'seeking breakthrough' or 'gaining power' would be a psychological leap beyond direct context. Only behavior is visible; motive is ambiguous.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed package focuses on individual-level conflicts among Gu Immortals (乐土, 方源, 巨阳仙尊, 星宿仙尊) and their personal alliances/enmities. No named collective actors (factions, clans, families, halls, shops, or organizations) appear in the seed entities or relations. The individuals are not explicitly tied to any named faction or group stake. 沈伤, 陆畏因, 气海老祖 are also individuals without collective group labels. Cannot extract a source-backed faction/group pressure candidate.
IF export filter quarantined 5 legacy old-schema branches.
